steps.title: Locate the question the passage explicitly poses
steps.content: After noting that most US English-speaking communities have begun using "y'all," the passage asks: "What explains its rise in popularity?" The rest of the passage then answers that question by explaining that "y'all" fills a gap — English lacks a dedicated plural "you" — making it more precise. That stated question is the one the text is attempting to answer.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• Choice A — How many other plural versions of the pronoun "you" are there in English, besides "y'all"?: The text mentions only "you" as a competing form and never counts or compares other plural pronouns. ✗
  • Choice B — Why has the pronoun "y'all" become more widely used in the US?: This restates the passage's own explicit question and is directly answered by the explanation about precision. ✓
  • Choice C — When was the first recorded use of the pronoun "y'all" in the English language?: The text gives a general geographic origin but never discusses when the pronoun was first recorded. ✗
  • Choice D — Is "y'all" commonly used in English-speaking regions of the world besides the US?: The text limits its scope to US English-speaking communities and says nothing about international usage. ✗
That matches Choice B — Why has the pronoun "y'all" become more widely used in the US?.
